mechanism_narrative: The continuous 3-month change in FEDFUNDS drives equity discount rates: lower or falling FEDFUNDS reduces the required return on equities, so more negative FEDFUNDS momentum predicts positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if lower 3-month FEDFUNDS changes do not produce higher 20-day S&P 500 returns than higher FEDFUNDS changes in walk-forward tests, or if rolling 3-year Rank IC is below 0.03.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp rise in UNRATE signals deteriorating labor income and higher recession risk, reducing expected corporate revenues and risk appetite; when unemployment deterioration is triggered, S&P 500 forward 20-day returns are negative.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises more than 0.3 percentage points over 3 months
failure_condition: Reject if triggered UNRATE increases are followed by positive 20-day S&P 500 returns in more than 60% of out-of-sample cases or if the mean triggered return is not below the unconditional mean.

mechanism_narrative: Rising RSAFS combined with very low FEDFUNDS identifies demand expansion supported by cheap financing; the joint condition raises expected earnings without immediate discount-rate pressure, producing positive 60-day S&P 500 returns.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS 3-month growth is positive and FEDFUNDS is below 1.0
regime_hint: post-shock demand recovery with accommodative policy
failure_condition: Reject if the joint RSAFS-positive and FEDFUNDS-below-1.0 condition fails to outperform the unconditional 60-day return in walk-forward tests, or if either single proxy fully explains the effect.

mechanism_narrative: Positive INDPRO momentum signals improving production volumes and operating leverage for listed firms; higher 3-month INDPRO growth predicts positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if positive INDPRO momentum has a nonpositive coefficient in out-of-sample 60-day return regressions or if rolling 3-year Rank IC is below 0.03.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating CPIAUCSL while FEDFUNDS remains pinned near zero creates policy catch-up risk; the combination raises expected future discount rates and compresses equity multiples, producing negative 20-day S&P 500 returns.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL 6-month annualized growth crosses above 4.0 while FEDFUNDS is below 0.25
failure_condition: Reject if the triggered CPIAUCSL-FEDFUNDS condition is followed by positive 20-day S&P 500 returns in more than 60% of walk-forward cases or if returns are not lower than baseline.

mechanism_narrative: A downside break in HOUST signals weakening rate-sensitive household formation and construction demand; when starts roll below trend, future cyclical earnings expectations decline and S&P 500 60-day returns are negative.
candidate_trigger: HOUST crosses below its 6-month moving average after being above it in the prior month
failure_condition: Reject if HOUST downside crossings do not produce below-median 60-day S&P 500 returns out of sample, or if the average triggered return is positive after transaction-lag alignment.

mechanism_narrative: Strong PAYEMS growth combined with a nonrising UNRATE signals durable labor income and resilient consumer demand; the joint labor-strength condition predicts positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS 3-month change exceeds 300 and UNRATE is flat or lower over the same 3 months
failure_condition: Reject if the joint PAYEMS-UNRATE condition fails to deliver positive 20-day returns in at least 55% of out-of-sample triggers or if its mean return is below the unconditional mean.

mechanism_narrative: Very low UNRATE combined with accelerating CPIAUCSL identifies wage-inflation pressure and imminent policy repricing; this late-cycle mix compresses equity multiples and predicts negative S&P 500 returns over 5 trading days.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE crosses below 4.0 while CPIAUCSL 6-month annualized growth exceeds 4.0
failure_condition: Reject if triggered observations are followed by nonnegative 5-day S&P 500 returns in more than 60% of out-of-sample cases or if the triggered return distribution is not below baseline.
